goal: 给仓库增加一个 readme 对应的中文版本，同时其他地方不应该出现中文了

items.title: Add Chinese README With Locale Guard
items.body:
- [ ] Add `README.zh-CN.md` as a Chinese translation of the current `README.md`, preserving the same structure, command names, config keys, links, status, and license details.
      Add an English-only language link from `README.md` to `README.zh-CN.md`, and a backlink from the Chinese file to `README.md`.
      Keep Han-script text allowed only in `README.zh-CN.md`; no source, tests, docs, package metadata, changelog text, or CLI output should contain Chinese.
      Add focused tests that scan tracked text files for Han characters outside `README.zh-CN.md` and assert package publishing metadata includes the localized README.
      Include `README.zh-CN.md` in package publishing metadata so the localized README ships with npm package contents.
      Update `CHANGELOG.md` under `## Unreleased`. Tests included.
items.rationale: Minimal path: this is one docs file, one English README link, package metadata, and a narrow invariant test. The repo currently has no Han-script matches, so the guard is cheap and directly enforces the operator's boundary. GitHub documents that root READMEs are surfaced to repository visitors and relative links are supported: https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/managing-your-repositorys-settings-and-features/customizing-your-repository/about-readmes. npm documents root README handling and package file inclusion rules: https://docs.npmjs.com/about-package-readme-files/ and https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v11/configuring-npm/package-json#files.

items.title: Chinese README Localization Boundary
items.body:
- [ ] Add `README.zh-Hans.md` as the sole Chinese documentation surface: a faithful Simplified Chinese version of `README.md`, with the English README remaining canonical.
      Add compact language links at the top of both READMEs using relative links, and include `README.zh-Hans.md` in the npm package `files` list.
      Add a focused test that scans tracked repo files and fails if Han characters appear outside `README.zh-Hans.md`.
      Keep all other docs, source, tests, templates, and CHANGELOG text in English.
      Update `CHANGELOG.md` under `## Unreleased`.
items.rationale: Architecture-first fit: this creates a clear localization boundary (`README.zh-Hans.md` plus an allowlist test) instead of relying on convention. GitHub documents README files as the surfaced repository entry point and recommends relative links for repository-local navigation (https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/managing-your-repositorys-settings-and-features/customizing-your-repository/about-readmes). W3C’s language-tag guidance identifies `zh-Hans` as Simplified Chinese and ties language tags to BCP 47, making the filename explicit and durable (https://www.w3.org/International/articles/language-tags/).

items.title: Chinese README with language guard
items.body:
- [ ] Add `README.zh-CN.md` as the Simplified Chinese counterpart to `README.md`, preserving the same section order, command names, examples, and links where applicable.
      Add an English-only translation link in `README.md` that points to `README.zh-CN.md`, and include the translated file in the published package surface.
      Add a docs test that compares the README heading outline and required command names between the English and Chinese files so the translation cannot silently drift.
      Add a docs test that scans tracked first-party text files and fails on Han characters outside `README.zh-CN.md`, excluding generated/vendor outputs only.
      Update `CHANGELOG.md` under `## Unreleased` in English. Tests included.
items.rationale: Risk-first: the main failure modes are translation drift, package omission, and Chinese text leaking into source, docs, or CLI strings. The repo currently has a single English README, no Han characters in first-party files, and package.json only lists README.md in files, so this candidate adds the translation plus executable guardrails before expanding any broader localization surface.
